name: Demo Charging (OCPP 1.6)
description: OCPP 1.6 §4.9 lifecycle demo. Stays in Available after boot until the CSMS sends RemoteStartTransaction; from there the domain auto-drives Preparing → Charging → Finishing → Available. Use with --scenario-template-file to apply to every connector of a multi-connector CP.

data.label: Wait for BootNotification.conf
data.description: §4.2 — give the CSMS time to Accept the BootNotification before exercising the connector. Until then the connector stays Unavailable.

data.label: Wait for RemoteStartTransaction
data.description: §5.11 — connector sits in Available until the CSMS sends RemoteStartTransaction.req. The forwarded idTag flows into the next Transaction node.

data.label: StartTransaction.req
data.description: §4.7 — sends StartTransaction.req with the idTag from RemoteStart. The domain auto-transitions Available → Preparing on send, then Preparing → Charging when the CSMS responds Accepted.

data.label: MeterValues (Sample.Periodic, stop at target SoC)
data.description: §5.9 — periodic MeterValues.req with Energy.Active.Import.Register. Increment matches an accelerated 150 kW DC fast-charge curve (1.25 kWh per 30 s). Stops automatically when the EV reaches evSettings.targetSoc.

data.label: StopTransaction.req (reason=Local)
data.description: §4.8 / §7.36 — StopTransaction.req. The domain transitions Charging → Finishing automatically; autoResetToAvailable then drops the connector back to Available.
